title: Railway Freight Yard: Budget-Constrained Motive-Power Routing
strategies: trivial: all-cheapest policy -- assign every cut its base yard switcher (option 0); always feasible, reproduces the evaluator baseline -> ~0.1

greedy: value/fuel-density greedy that upgrades each cut STRAIGHT to its single highest-value option; ignores diminishing returns and the multiple-choice structure, wasting budget on inefficient heavy units -> well above baseline but sub-optimal

strong: Lagrangian relaxation -- bisect a fuel price lambda so per-cut argmax(value - lambda*fuel) meets the budget, then greedily repair leftover budget with the most fuel-efficient upgrades; multiple-choice aware across the whole trace, near-optimal with headroom (~0.55-0.85)
invalid: assign every cut its heaviest option -> total fuel always exceeds the budget -> infeasible on every instance -> 0
